steps.n: Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show who does the action, like changing 'exceed' to 'exceeder'

steps.m: those who go beyond limits (object of أَهْلَكْنَا/we destroyed)
steps.n: The ين ending changes it from 'one person who goes beyond limits' to 'many people who go beyond limits', and shows they are the ones being destroyed in this ayah
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis. This combination introduces the verb أَنزَلْنَآ (We sent down) to emphasize that the action definitely happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'cause to descend' instead of just 'descend'
Adding نَا at the end shows 'we' did the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: we sent down (connected to إِلَيْكُمْ - to you all)
steps.n: The long alif at the end is a writing style that shows this word connects to the next word إِلَيْكُمْ (to you all)
This is a preposition showing direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix

steps.m: to you all (connected to أَنزَلْنَآ - we sent down)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to أَنزَلْنَآ (we sent down) to show what was sent down to them.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the vowel pattern and added alif to make it into a noun for the thing that results from writing

steps.m: a book (object of أَنزَلْنَآ - We sent down)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sent down in the sentence. The word is indefinite (any book) and is being acted upon by the verb أَنزَلْنَآ (We sent down).
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'

steps.m: in it (referring to كِتٰبًا - the book)
steps.n: Added هِ (meaning 'it') to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to the book (كِتٰبًا) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: remembrance, mention, reminder (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'to remind' into a noun meaning 'remembrance' by adding vowel marks. The damma ending shows it's doing the action.

steps.m: your (plural) reminder/mention (referring to what Allah has sent down in the book)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ meaning 'your (plural)' - addressing multiple people. This connects to the earlier إِلَيْكُمْ (to you all) in the ayah, showing the book and reminder are for the same group.
This particle turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention to what follows

steps.m: is it then that...? / do then...?
steps.n: Added فَ which shows this question follows from what was mentioned before - in this case, about the Book containing their mention/reminder

steps.m: then will you not? (connected to تَعْقِلُونَ - use reason)
steps.n: Added لَا (not) to make this a negative rhetorical question. The full meaning is 'then will you not use reason?' - expressing surprise that they don't understand despite having been given a Book with their reminder in it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense and added 'you' as the doer

steps.m: you all use your minds (addressing the disbelievers in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show that this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, Allah is asking the disbelievers why they don't use their minds to understand.

steps.m: how many (introduces a question about the number of towns destroyed)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about quantity

steps.m: and how many (connects to previous verses about destroyed towns)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this statement with previous verses. This introduces a rhetorical question about the many towns that were destroyed.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for adding 'we' ending

steps.m: we broke/destroyed (main verb of the sentence)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is the main verb describing what we did to قَرْيَةٍ (a town/village).
Basic preposition showing where the breaking/destroying started. In this ayah, it connects 'We destroyed' (قَصَمْنَا) with 'town' (قَرْيَةٍ) to show which towns were destroyed.
The word 'min' means 'from' and connects words together - here it links 'village' to 'We destroyed'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters. The fatha and sukun show this is becoming a noun.
Added ة to make it into a specific place where people gather and settle
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any village/town, not a specific one. In this ayah, it refers to a village that was wrongdoing (ظَالِمَةً).
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine. This matches with قَرْيَةٍ (town/village) which is feminine in Arabic.
Changed to active participle meaning 'wronging'
Added fatha, alif, and kasra to show who does the action
Added ة to show this describes a female
Added tanween fatha to show this describes the town (قَرْيَةٍ) as being wrongdoing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'cause something to happen'
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for adding the 'we' suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic word meaning 'after'
Added a small line (fatha) above the last letter to show it connects to the word after it
Added the feminine pronoun ها to show this is happening after the town (قَرْيَةٍ) mentioned earlier
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'people' or 'folk'. The و becomes silent with sukun.

steps.m: a people (object of قَصَمْنَا - we destroyed)
steps.n: Added tanween fatah (ًا) to show this word is receiving the action of destruction mentioned in the verse. It's what was destroyed.
Changed the hamza to a madda form for easier pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used as an object
Added the masculine plural ending ين with fatha to show this is the object of the verb 'we produced' in the ayah
This is a timing word that shows when something happens

steps.m: and when, then when (connects to أَحَسُّوا۟)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happens right after something else. In this ayah, it connects to when they felt (أَحَسُّوا۟) something.
The آ at the end is just a different way of writing the same word to show emphasis on timing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The middle letter gets a shadda (doubled) to show the feeling is stronger or more intense
Adding 'أ' at the start makes it mean 'to cause/make someone feel' instead of just 'feel'. Like changing 'feel' to 'make feel'
Added وا to show many people did the action. The word بَأْسَنَآ (our punishment) after it shows what they sensed.
Changes from verb to noun meaning a single instance of power/might
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb أَحَسُّوا (they felt/perceived)

steps.m: our might, our power (as object of أَحَسُّوا)
steps.n: Added نَآ at the end to show this might belongs to 'us' (Allah). The word is being acted upon by the verb أَحَسُّوا (they felt/perceived)
This is a basic time word that isn't derived from a root. It shows when something happens.
Added kasra (ِ) under the إ to help with pronunciation
Added fatha (َ) on ذ to help with pronunciation
Added alif (ا) at the end to make it mean 'when' in relation to a specific event. In this ayah, it refers to when they felt Allah's punishment.
Basic pronoun form meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males or mixed gender
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject form of the pronoun
This shows something is coming from or moving away from something else
Added ها to show what is being moved away from. In this ayah, it refers back to the punishment (بَأْسَنَآ) that was mentioned.
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (إِذَا), making the pronunciation stronger
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action in present time

steps.m: they are running away quickly (subject of إِذَا هُم)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This describes what 'they' (هُم) are doing in the ayah when they sense Our punishment.
This is the basic negative word that means 'no' or 'not'. In this ayah, it's used with the verb تَرْكُضُوا۟ (run away) to tell people 'do not run away'

steps.m: do not (connected to تَرْكُضُوا۟ - run away)
steps.n: Negative command word that connects to 'run away' - tells people not to do something
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. The small mark after وا shows this is a command.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he returned' to 'return!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels to make it a command to one person
Added وا at the end to make it a command for multiple people
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase لَا تَرْكُضُوا۟ (do not flee)
This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. It's one of the core words in Arabic that isn't built from a root.
Added an alif khanjariya (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it points towards what you were given luxury in (أُتْرِفْتُمْ).
A word used to ask questions or connect parts of sentences
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: what (referring to أُتْرِفْتُمْ - you were given luxury)
steps.n: Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound. In this ayah, it's asking about what they were given luxury in.

steps.m: to live in ease and luxury

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he was made to live in luxury
steps.n: Changed to passive form by adding أُ at start and changing vowels to show something was done to someone

steps.m: you all were made to live in luxury
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show this happened to multiple people being addressed
Basic preposition showing location or containment

steps.m: in it (referring to what they were given luxuries in)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix هِ meaning 'it' to show what is being referred to. This connects to the previous phrase 'مَآ أُتْرِفْتُمْ' (what you were given luxuries in)
Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens, like how 'write' becomes 'writing place'
Added ا after second letter and ِ before last letter to show many dwellings
Added كُمْ at the end to show it belongs to multiple people being spoken to
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
A word that shows something might happen

Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to تُسْـَٔلُونَ (you are questioned) in the next ayah, showing these are the same people being addressed.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added تُ at start and changed vowels to show present passive voice - something is happening to you
Added ونَ at the end to show this is happening to multiple people
Changes vowel marks to indicate passive voice
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense
Changed from قَوَلَ by replacing weak letter و with ا
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is connected to what follows - they are the ones who say 'يٰوَيْلَنَآ' (Oh, woe to us!)
This is the basic word for expressing misfortune or destruction
Added يٰ at the beginning to call out to the concept of woe, like saying 'Oh!' in English

steps.m: Oh, woe to us! (exclamation of those speaking in قَالُوا)
steps.n: Added نَآ at the end to show this woe belongs to or affects 'us'. This is being said by the people mentioned in قَالُوا (they said)
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word 'indeed'
Adding ا at the end makes this mean 'we'. This word connects to كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah, showing who 'we' refers to.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fathas) to make it mean 'he existed' in the past
Because و is a weak letter, it drops out and the vowels change to match. This is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle).
Adding نَا at the end (not just ا) changes 'he was' to 'we were'. The نَّ is doubled because it comes after إِنَّا in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the middle letter to alif and added kasra to show someone who does the action. This is the predicate (describes what we were) after كُنَّا (we were).
The ين ending makes it plural and shows it's affected by كُنَّا (we were). The alif got stretched (ٰ) when combined with ين.
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before. Here it connects to زَالَت (ceased) to say 'Then they did not cease...'
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb
Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is feminine singular. This word describes what happened to دَعْوَىٰهُمْ (their claim/plea) in this ayah.

steps.m: that (feminine, pointing to something far away)
steps.n: This is a basic pointing word used to indicate something far away that is feminine. It's like saying 'that' in English when pointing to something far.

steps.m: that (feminine, pointing to something far away)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ت sound with a shadda. This happens because the word comes after فَ, which often causes the next word to have a shadda.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
The verb 'he called' becomes a noun meaning 'claim' by adding ى at the end

steps.m: their claim (referring to those who denied the truth)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows that the claim belongs to them. In this ayah, it refers to the claims/excuses of those who denied the truth.
Basic form of the word that means reaching a limit
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: until (connects to جَعَلْنٰهُمْ - we made them)
steps.n: Adding the alif maqsurah (ىٰ) at the end makes this word work as a preposition meaning 'until'. In this ayah, it connects to the next phrase 'we made them', showing how long something continued.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نَا to show 'we' did this action

steps.m: we made them (into something - refers to خٰمِدِينَ/lifeless)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who was affected by the action. The word connects to خٰمِدِينَ which tells what they were made into.

steps.m: cut down/harvested (something that was affected by cutting/harvesting)
steps.n: Added ي between the last two root letters and vowel marks to show this describes something that was cut down or harvested

steps.m: cut down/harvested (object of جَعَلْنٰ - we made them)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing what happened to 'them' - Allah made them like something that was completely cut down
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under middle letter to show who or what has this quality
Added ين to show multiple people/things having this quality
Added fatha at end to show accusative case
Basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions/statements) or 'not' (for negation). In this ayah it means 'not' since it's negating the idea of playful creation.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. Here it negates the word لٰعِبِينَ (playing/in play), saying Allah did not create things in play.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نا changes it from 'he created' to 'we created'

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high above
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the sky' instead of just 'sky'

steps.m: the sky/heaven (object of خَلَقْنَا - we created)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb 'we created' (خَلَقْنَا)

steps.m: to be wide and spread out

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make the common word for earth
Added ال to make it specific
Added و to connect it to السَّمَآءَ (the sky) mentioned before it

steps.m: and the earth/ground (object of خَلَقْنَا - we created)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object being created in خَلَقْنَا (we created)
This is a negation particle that makes the verb خَلَقْنَا (we created) negative. In this context it means 'we did not create'
The و connects this negation to the previous statement, making it 'and we did not create'

steps.m: to be between or separate things

steps.n: Added fatha and sukoon to make the basic word meaning 'between'
Added a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end to show this word connects to other words in the sentence

steps.m: between them both (referring to السَّمَآء and الْأَرْض)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this means 'between them both', referring to the sky and earth mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to play or act without serious purpose

steps.n: Basic past tense verb showing someone played or acted without serious purpose
Changed from 'played' to 'player' - like changing from 'ran' to 'runner'

steps.m: players/those who act without purpose (describing how Allah did NOT create the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Made plural by adding ين. This word relates to خَلَقْنَا (we created) in the ayah, showing that Allah did not create things in play or jest.
This is a conditional particle that always talks about impossible or unfulfilled conditions in the past
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at start makes it mean 'to cause/make something happen'
Adding نا changes it from 'he' to 'we'
The alif after نا makes the sound longer

steps.m: that (connects 'if We had intended' to 'We take')
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two ideas together. Here it connects أَرَدْنَآ (We had intended) with نَّتَّخِذَ (We take)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added ت and merged it with first letter to mean 'took for himself/adopted' - like taking something and keeping it
Added ن at beginning to make it 'we' instead of 'he', and changed middle vowel to kasra (ِ) to show present tense
The ن has a shadda because it merged with another ن before it, like saying 'nn' together quickly

steps.m: to amuse oneself, to be diverted (from serious things)

steps.n: Root letters combined with vowels to make a noun meaning 'amusement'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being taken/adopted in the phrase 'if We had wished to take a diversion'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The ت is doubled (has shadda) to show this is done with more effort than just 'taking'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action and made it about ourselves
Added لَ (laam) at start to mean 'surely/definitely' - shows what would happen after 'if' (لو)

steps.m: surely we would take it/him (referring to لَهْوًا - diversion)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what is being taken (refers back to لَهْوًا - diversion), and changed spelling of alif to match Uthmani script
This is a helper word that shows something coming from somewhere. In this ayah, it connects to لَّدُنَّآ (Our presence) to show where something would come from.
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: to be present, to be near

steps.n: The vowels change the word to express the meaning of being near or present
The doubled letter at the end makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: near, in the presence of (with emphasis and completion)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end completes the word structure

steps.m: near, in the presence of (as an adverb)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end makes it work like an adverb
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it connects to كُنَّا (we were) to express 'if we were...'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The middle weak letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to ُ with a sukun on the second letter
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is connected to فٰعِلِينَ (doers) in the ayah, showing who the doers are.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he did) to show who does the action - like changing 'ran' to 'runner'. The last part about accusative case isn't needed for understanding the basic change.
Makes the word plural and shows it describes 'we' in كُنَّا (we were)
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is in the accusative case

steps.m: No! Rather... (introduces what follows in نَقْذِفُ)
steps.n: This is a special word that does two things: 1) It rejects what was said before, and 2) It introduces the truth that comes after. Like saying 'No! Actually...' in English. Here it introduces the truth about how Allah deals with falsehood.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

The ن at the beginning shows 'we' are doing the action. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ), and the third letter gets a kasra (ِ). The ُ at the end shows this is happening in the present/future. This word is connected to يَدْمَغُهُۥ (it crushes it) in the ayah, showing what happens after we throw.
Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make the noun 'truth'
Adding ال makes it specific - not just any truth, but THE truth

steps.m: with/by the truth (used with نَقْذِفُ - we strike)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows how the truth is used as a tool or means. This word explains how Allah strikes/throws (نَقْذِفُ) in this ayah.
The word has a double letter (shadda) and kasra ending because it comes after بِ (bi-)
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or against something else. In this ayah, it connects the truth (الحق) being thrown against falsehood (الباطل).
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from verb (became false) to noun (something false)
Makes it specific by adding 'the'
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows عَلَى (upon) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense. This verb describes what الْحَقّ (the truth) does after being thrown.
Added هُ to show what is being crushed (الْبَاطِل/falsehood)

steps.m: and then it crushes it completely
steps.n: Added conjunction prefix فَ to connect with previous statement
This is a particle that introduces something happening suddenly or at a specific time. In this ayah, it shows what happens right after the truth strikes falsehood.
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happens right after the previous action (the truth striking falsehood). It connects to يَدْمَغُهُۥ (it crushes it) in the previous part of the ayah.
This is a basic pronoun that refers back to الْبٰطِلِ (falsehood) in this ayah. It's used to show which thing we're talking about.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
This pattern shows something that is actively vanishing. The word describes هُوَ (it) in the ayah.
Adding tanween dammah (ٌ) makes it indefinite, meaning it's talking about vanishing in a general way
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Added ُ at the end to help connect with the next word (ٱلْوَيْلُ) smoothly
This is the basic form of the word meaning severe punishment or destruction. It's often used as a threat or warning of bad consequences.
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific destruction or punishment rather than just any destruction.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is the subject in the sentence. It connects to لَكُمُ (for you) in the ayah.
Starting with the two separate words: مِنْ meaning 'from' and مَا meaning 'what'

steps.m: from what (describing تَصِفُونَ - what you describe)
steps.n: When مِنْ comes before مَا, the ن blends into the م, making it stronger (shown by ّ). This is like how in English 'in memory' sounds like 'im-memory' when spoken quickly.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at start changes it from past to present tense and makes it mean 'you' or 'she'
Adding ون shows that many people are doing the describing
This is a basic preposition showing possession or belonging. It introduces who something belongs to.
Adding و connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah
The هُۥ shows this belongs to Him (Allah). The ۥ mark after ه shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.

steps.m: whoever/who (refers to those in السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to those in the heavens and earth.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to 'the heavens' to show who is in them.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Makes the word plural by adding the feminine plural ending ات
Adding ال makes it definite. This word follows فِى (in) in the ayah.
Adding kasra at the end marks this noun as genitive case
The fatha mark on أ turns the root letters into the common noun meaning earth/land
The kasra at the end shows this word is being used after a preposition or as a possessive

steps.m: whoever (refers to the beings/creatures mentioned in وَلَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: Basic word meaning 'whoever' - used to refer to intelligent beings. In this ayah it refers back to the creatures in the heavens mentioned in the previous phrase.

steps.m: and whoever (connects this phrase to the previous description of beings in the heavens)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this to the previous statement about beings in the heavens. The و shows these beings are an additional group being described.
This is a special word used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added هُۥ at the end to show this means 'with him'. The ۥ mark is just to show the pronunciation of the ه
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. In this ayah, it negates يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (they become arrogant), making it mean 'they do not become arrogant'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
The ُ (damma) shows this is about having the quality of being great
Added است at the beginning to show someone making themselves act in a big/proud way
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This word is the subject of يَتَّخِذُونَكَ (they take you) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. In this ayah, it negates يَتَّخِذُونَكَ (they take you), making it mean 'they do not take you'
Added اِ at start and تَّ after first letter to show doing something for yourself

steps.m: they take for themselves (group of people)
steps.n: Added ون at end to show many people are doing it
Added كَ at end to show you are being taken/treated this way. In this ayah, the disbelievers are taking/treating the Prophet (peace be upon him) as a joke.
This is a basic connecting word that shows an exception. In this ayah, it connects 'they take you' (يَتَّخِذُونَكَ) with 'in mockery' (هُزُوًا), meaning 'they only take you in mockery'.

steps.m: to mock or make fun of

steps.n: Adding damma vowels changes this from a verb into a noun meaning the act of mocking
Adding tanween fatha makes this word indefinite and shows it's being used as an object

steps.m: as a mockery (object of يَتَّخِذُونَكَ - they take you)
steps.n: The ء (hamza) changes to و (waw) when it has a damma (u sound), making it easier to pronounce. The ًا (tanween fatha) shows this is the object - what is being mocked.
This is the demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' - it points to something nearby
Added the questioning particle أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question. In this ayah, the disbelievers are mockingly asking 'Is this the one who speaks about your gods?'
This is the base form of the relative pronoun that refers to a singular masculine person or thing
Added ال with shadda to make it a definite relative pronoun. In this ayah, it refers back to هٰذَا (this person) who the disbelievers are mocking.
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha

steps.m: he mentions/remembers (the subject of this verb is الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ - those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, damma on middle root letter, and damma at end. In this ayah, this word describes what the disbelievers are doing.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes the action into a noun meaning the one who is worshipped
Changes the spelling to prepare for plural formation
Adding ة makes this word feminine, like saying 'goddess' instead of 'god'
Adds تَ and كُمْ at the end - تَ connects the word to كُمْ which means 'your' (talking to many people)
Added damma to show basic pronoun form
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This pronoun refers to those who reject the mention of الرَّحْمٰنِ (The Most Merciful).
Changed from a basic root meaning 'remember' into a noun meaning 'remembrance' by adding the vowel marks
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with/by' and ِ at the end to show this connects to the next word الرَّحْمٰنِ (the Most Merciful)
Creates an intensive form showing complete and perfect mercy
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows the word is affected by a preposition or possession
This is a personal pronoun that refers to multiple people (all male or mixed group). In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier who mock the Prophet.

steps.m: to cover up or reject truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to describe someone who does this action as their characteristic
Changed the alif to an alif with overbar (spelling variation)
Added ون to show this refers to multiple people who do this action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that this is something that happened to someone/something rather than something they did. In this ayah, الْإِنسٰنُ (mankind/human) is what was created.

steps.m: to feel comfort or companionship with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun referring to humans as a group, showing how humans are social beings who find comfort with each other
Changes from humans as a group to referring to an individual human

steps.m: the human being (subject of خُلِقَ - was created)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this is the subject of 'was created' (خُلِقَ). The alif became longer (ٰ) when connecting the word parts.

steps.m: from (showing what عَجَلٍ is made of)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing what something is made of or comes from. Here it connects to عَجَلٍ (haste) to show what humans are created from.

steps.m: from (showing what عَجَلٍ is made of)
steps.n: Added sukun (ْ) on the ن to show the word ends here. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he rushed) to a noun (haste) and added tanwin kasra because it comes after مِنْ (from)
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he saw'

Adds و with small high waw to add emphasis
Adds the attached pronoun meaning 'you all' (masculine plural)
Forms the basic noun meaning 'sign' by changing the root structure. The hamza and alif combine into آ.

Adds first person possessive pronoun suffix ي to show these are Allah's signs. This word is what will be shown in the phrase 'I will show you My signs'
Added fatha to show this is the basic negative particle that stops something from happening. Here it's telling people not to rush.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before - because humans were created hasty, they shouldn't rush.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding است at the beginning shows seeking or wanting to do the action
Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you' and vowel changes

steps.m: you all seek to rush me
steps.n: Added plural 'you all' and 'me' endings
Adds يَ at start to show 'he' does action in present time
Adds masculine plural ending to indicate multiple subjects

steps.m: and they speak/say (asking about the promise)
steps.n: Added 'و' at start to connect this with what came before it. In this ayah, they are asking about when Allah's promise will come.
This is a question word used to ask about time, like saying 'When will this happen?' It doesn't come from a root.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly - MA-TA

steps.m: when (asking about time) (question in وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ - and they say, 'When?')
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound at the end. In this ayah, the disbelievers are asking 'When will this promise come?' in a way that shows they don't believe it will happen.
Basic demonstrative word that points to something nearby (like pointing and saying "this")
Added the small alif (ٰ) above ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation. This word is what they are talking about in يَقُولُونَ (they say)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun. The fatha on و and sukun on ع show this is a noun meaning 'a promise'
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific. This is the subject of the question 'When will this promise (come)?'
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the question 'When will this promise come?'

This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if you are' (إِن كُنتُمْ) with what comes before it - people asking 'When will this promise come?' The full meaning is 'if you are truthful (about wanting to know)'

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Command form ('be!') - shortened by dropping middle letter and changing vowels

steps.m: you all were (asking about الْوَعْدُ - the promise)
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show this refers to a group of people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from action to describe someone who tells the truth. This describes the subject of كنتم (you were/are).

steps.m: if (in a hypothetical situation that didn't happen)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it introduces a hypothetical situation about the disbelievers, connecting to يَعْلَمُ (they know/will know)
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to show ongoing action
Same word - shows who is doing the knowing (he)
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and turn it into 'those who'
Added shadda on ل and fatha at the end. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) to tell us who did the disbelieving.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word is part of what the people 'know' (يَعْلَمُ) in this verse.
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Adding the kasra (ِ) vowel makes this into a proper noun meaning 'time'

steps.m: when (referring to the time when the disbelievers will not be able to ward off the Fire)

Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation

Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action

steps.m: they hold back (subject of لَوْ يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This word is part of what the disbelievers would know if they knew.

Added vowel marks to form basic noun meaning 'face'
Changed from single face (wajh) to plural faces (wujūh) by changing the vowels inside the word
Added kasra to show possession
Added pronoun suffix meaning 'their' with extra vowel at end for pronunciation

This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it

This is a basic preposition that shows something moving away from something else. In this ayah, it connects 'their faces' (وُجُوهِهِمُ) to the verb 'they will not ward off' (يَكُفُّونَ), showing what they can't keep away.
The root letters show the basic idea of something being visible or on the back/surface
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'back' by adding vowel marks (fatHa and sukun)
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural (more than one back)
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِمْ to show the backs belong to them (a group of people)
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates the verb يَكُفُّونَ (they prevent/ward off) in this ayah.

This is a standalone pronoun used as the subject of the verb يُنصَرُونَ (they are helped/supported). The هُمْ shows we're talking about a group of males or a mixed group.
This is the basic meaning of the root letters

steps.m: it/she comes (suddenly, as indicated by بَغْتَةً after it)

Added هِم at the end to show who receives the action (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb

Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ at start, and ُ at end shows it's doing the action now

steps.m: to be able, to obey willingly

steps.m: to send back or return something

Basic particle word that refers to 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers back to what the disbelievers used to mock.
Added shadda for emphasis. This emphasizes what is being referred to - the mockery that will come back to affect those who mocked.
The و is a weak letter that affects how the word is formed

Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form

Added a kasra ( ِ ) at the end because بِ makes the word after it take this sound
Root means bright flowing - adding alif makes it into 'daytime' when light flows
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at beginning to connect it with the previous word (night)
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition بِ in بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ (by night and day)
Basic preposition showing something comes from or originates from somewhere
Added fatha to help connect smoothly to the next word الرَّحْمٰنِ (the Most Merciful). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The ان ending makes it stronger - like saying 'full of mercy' instead of just 'merciful'
Adding ال (the) makes it 'The Most Merciful' and doubles the ر sound (shown by the shadda mark)
Added a small line ( ِ ) under the last letter to show it belongs to/is owned by something

steps.m: away from (shows turning away from ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِم - remembrance of their Lord)

Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act or instance of remembering/mentioning'
The kasra shows this word is connected to 'their Lord' (رَبِّهِم) which comes right after it
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters

Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning
Added هُمْ to show this applies to 'them' (referring to the false gods mentioned in ءَالِهَةٌ in this ayah)

Added damma and kasra to form the preposition meaning 'besides' or 'other than'
Added kasra to connect with following pronoun

steps.m: to be able, to obey willingly

Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'

steps.m: to enjoy or benefit from something

steps.m: he said (introducing what he said to لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ - to his father and his people)
steps.n: The و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two letters that also have fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Connected the root letters together. The weak letter و is dropped in this form.
Added ي at the end with kasra before it to mean 'my father'
Added لِ at beginning to show direction/purpose. This word is connected to قَالَ (he said) in the ayah.

steps.m: to his father (object of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added هِ to show it belongs to him. This word is what's being said in the phrase 'when he said to his father'.

steps.m: to stand firmly, rise up, establish
steps.n: This root relates to standing or rising
The meaning shifted from standing to a group of people who stand together as a community
Adds -i ending to show it belongs to someone (like 'my people' or 'people of')
Adds هِ to show these are his people
Adds coordinating conjunction و meaning 'and'
This is a question word used to ask about things. In this ayah, Ibrahim is asking his father and people about their statues.
Basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ذا to show something is nearby
Changed to feminine form by adding ِهِ at the end and alif above ذ. This word points to التَّمَاثِيلُ (the statues) in this ayah.

steps.m: to represent or be similar to something
steps.n: This root relates to similarity or resemblance

steps.m: statue/idol (something made to look like something else)
steps.n: Creates a noun meaning something that represents or looks like something else
Changes the word to plural form by rearranging vowels and adding ي
Adds the definite article. The ت gets a shadda because it combines with the ل of ال
Adds U-ending because this word is what/who is being devoted to (the thing people are worshipping)
This is the basic feminine relative pronoun used to refer back to feminine nouns
Added ال with shadda to make it definite and connect it to what came before. This refers back to التَّمَاثِيلُ (the statues) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added a small alif (ٓ) at the end to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
This is the basic form used to talk directly to one male person
Adding مْ at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of the preposition
Added هَا which shows this is happening to something feminine (either 'her' or 'it'). This word connects to عٰكِفُونَ (devoted) to show what they are devoted to.
This root means to stay in one place with dedication
Added vowels to make it past tense - 'he stayed devoted'
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people
Adds the nominative case ending to show it's the subject
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows one male person did the action in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic words

steps.m: they said (subject of the sentence before وَجَدْنَآ)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people (3 or more men) did the action. This word is connected to the next part of the ayah where they say what they found (وَجَدْنَآ).
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: we found (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word tells us who is speaking in the ayah.

steps.m: to care for/nurture (like a father does)

steps.n: Root meaning 'to nurture' becomes the simple word for 'father'
Changed the word to show many fathers instead of just one
Added 'our' to show these are our fathers. This word is what was found (object of وَجَدْنَآ - we found)
This is a preposition showing direction or ownership. Here it connects عٰبِدِينَ (worshippers) to what they are worshipping.

steps.m: for/to her/it (referring to idols they worship)
steps.n: Adding ها refers to something feminine (her/it). In this ayah, it refers to the idols they found their fathers worshipping.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to show who does the action
Changed the alif to a special alif (ٰ) for writing style
Added ين to show multiple worshippers. This is the object (what was found) in the phrase 'we found our fathers as worshippers of it'
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is the main verb that introduces what he said in لَقَدْ كُنتُمْ (you were indeed).
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
This is a basic particle that shows something is certain or definite, like saying 'indeed' in English

steps.m: certainly/indeed (emphasizing what كُنتُمْ says after it)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to make the meaning stronger. This word emphasizes the statement that comes after it (كُنتُمْ - 'you were')
The basic past tense form puts fathas (َ) on each letter
Changed to command form ('be!') by adding sukun and dropping the middle letter و
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past

steps.m: you all were (in ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ - clear error)
steps.n: Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end
This is the base form of the pronoun that refers to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: you all (subject of كُنتُمْ - you were)
steps.n: Added م with sukun at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This pronoun is the subject of the verb كُنتُمْ (you were) in this ayah.

steps.m: to nurture/care for as a father

steps.n: Root letters أ-ب-و became the noun 'father' with vowel marks
Changed from 'father' to plural 'fathers' by stretching the middle sound and adding a long ending
Added conjunction to connect to previous phrase about 'you and your ancestors'
Changed ending to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added suffix to show these are 'your' fathers, connecting to the previous phrase 'you were'
Basic preposition showing location or position
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it connects 'you were' (كُنتُمْ) to 'clear error' (ضَلٰلٍ) to say 'you were in clear error'

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from an action (going astray) to a noun meaning the state of being lost by adding alif
Added tanween kasra to show this word is indefinite and in genitive case

steps.m: a state of being lost/misguided (after في/in)
steps.n: The alif (ا) is written as a small vertical line (ٰ) above the second letter - both spellings mean the same thing
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'something that makes clear' instead of 'made clear'
Added emphasis to show how strong or definite the clarity is
Added -in ending to match with 'error' (ḍalāl) that it's describing in the verse
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed from قَوَلَ by replacing weak letter و with ا
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added optional marks (alif khanjariyah and small circle) that don't change meaning
Connected the root letters and added past tense vowels. The middle letter ي changes to ا due to being a weak letter.
Changed to second person past tense by adding تَ at the end and adjusting the vowels
Adding interrogative prefix أَ to make it a question
Adding first person plural pronoun suffix نَا meaning 'us'. This is a question form of the verb 'to come' (جاء) with the pronoun 'you' and 'us' attached.

steps.m: to be true, to be real, to be right

steps.n: The doubled letter (ّ) makes it into a noun meaning the concept itself
Adding ال makes it specific - 'the truth' not just any truth
Adding kasra (ِ) shows this word is affected by the word before it (بِ)

steps.m: or (in questions: 'are you one of those who play?')
steps.n: This is a special word that introduces the second part of a question. Here it connects two possibilities: 'Did you bring us the truth?' OR 'are you just playing with us?'
This is the basic part that means 'you' in Arabic
Adding تَ makes it specifically for talking to one male person. This word is being addressed by the speakers in قَالُوٓا۟ (they said).
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to a group
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱللَّاعِبِينَ (the players/jokers). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when speaking.
Basic past tense verb showing someone did this action
Changes from a verb (played) to describe someone who does the playing
Adding ين makes it plural (more than two). The kasra (ِ) before ين shows it's owned or follows 'from/of'
Adding ال makes it specific. The shadda (ّ) appears because ل from ال combines with the first ل of the word. Added tanwin (َ) at the end because it follows مِنَ.
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is a basic particle that introduces a correction or contradiction to what came before. Here it's correcting what was previously said.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
Shows this word is the subject (doer) of the verb قَالَ (he said) in this ayah
Adds كُمْ to show this Lord belongs to or nurtures all of you (plural)
Word already has the required shadda on ب, so no change needed
The doubled middle letter (shadda) transforms this from an action into someone/something that does that action very completely
The damma (ُ) shows this word is the main subject. In this ayah, it's part of رَّبُّكُمْ (your Lord)
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Makes the word plural by adding the feminine plural ending ات
Adding ال makes the noun definite
Adding kasra at the end makes it genitive case
The fatha mark on أ turns the root letters into the common noun meaning earth/land
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific. The ِ at the end shows it's being acted upon by the verb فَطَرَ (created/originated)
Added وَ (and) to connect this with ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before
Added ى to make it a relative pronoun. This turns a simple pointing word into one that connects descriptions to nouns.
Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to make it definite. In this ayah, it connects 'Lord of the heavens and earth' to the next phrase 'who created them.'

steps.m: he created (referring to the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb. In this ayah, this refers specifically to Allah creating the heavens and earth.

steps.m: he created them (referring to the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added هُنَّ at the end to show that the action was done to them (the heavens and earth, which are treated as feminine plural in Arabic)
Personal pronoun meaning 'I'. In this ayah, Ibrahim (peace be upon him) is referring to himself.

steps.m: and I (Ibrahim, connecting to his previous statement)
steps.n: Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this to Ibrahim's previous statement about Allah being the Lord of the heavens and earth
This is the basic form of the word that means being high or elevated
Adding the short 'a' vowels (fatha) turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to be high'

steps.m: upon, on (shows position relative to ذٰلِكُم)
steps.n: Adding the ىٰ at the end turns it into a preposition showing position. In this ayah, it shows the position relative to the word ذٰلِكُم (that)
This is the basic pointing word used to indicate something. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this form.
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something close ('this') to pointing to something far away ('that')
Adding كُم shows we're talking to multiple people. This word is connected to مِّنَ (from) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis on the م sound, making it stronger.
The fatha (َ) at the end helps connect this word to the next word الشَّاهِدِينَ (the witnesses), showing it means 'from the witnesses'
Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural. This shows multiple people who witness.
Added ال (the) at the beginning which combines with ش to become ٱلشَّ. The word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah.
Starting with the base divine name Allah
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added a small line (kasra) under the last letter to show it's part of an oath phrase like 'By Allah' or 'I swear by Allah'
Added تَ (ta) at the start to make it into 'I swear by Allah' - this تَ is a special letter used only for swearing by Allah

steps.m: And I swear by Allah (connected to لَأَكِيدَنَّ - I will surely plot)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this oath to what follows
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he plotted' to present tense 'I plot'
The لَ adds emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: I will most certainly plot/scheme (against أَصْنٰمَكُم)
steps.n: Added the emphatic نَّ ending which adds strong emphasis to an oath or promise
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a real word meaning one idol

Added كُم at the end to show these idols belong to multiple people being spoken to. The alif changes to a long alif (ٰ) and gets a fatha.

steps.m: after (أَن تُوَلُّوا۟ - that you turn away)
steps.n: Changed the root meaning 'distant' into the preposition 'after'. In this ayah, it refers to the time after the people turn away.
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence, like saying 'I want that you go' in English. Here it connects to the word تُوَلُّوا۟ (you turn away).
Added the fatha mark (َ) above أ to show how to pronounce it as 'a'
Changed the root letters to past tense by adding vowels and doubling the middle letter (shadda) to show he turned away strongly
Adding ت at the start shows the person did this action to themselves
Adding ُ (damma) changes it from active to passive voice
Added وا ending to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to mean 'one who' and changed vowels to show it's the doer of the action
Added ين to make it plural (more than two people)

Added فَ at the start to show this action happened right after Ibrahim broke the idols, connecting the sequence of events

steps.m: and then he made them (into جُذٰذًا)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was affected - the idols that Ibrahim broke
Added damma at start and fatha in middle to make it a plural noun for pieces
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action - these idols were made into broken pieces. Also added ٰ (long alif) for pronunciation.

This is a special word (particle) that doesn't come from a root. It shows that something is being excluded from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it's excluding the biggest idol from being broken.
Added shadda and alif at the end to complete the word's standard form. This is how this word always appears in Arabic.
Added ي between second and third letters to show this describes something that has this quality strongly

steps.m: big/large one (object of جَعَلَ - he made)

This is a basic particle that expresses hope or expectation about something happening

steps.m: perhaps they (referring to the idols that Ibrahim broke, mentioned in جُذٰذًا)
steps.n: Added هُمْ ('they/them') to the end to show who the hope is about. This 'they' refers to Ibrahim's people, not the idols, since idols can't return. He hoped his people would come back and see what he did.
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement toward something
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: to him, toward him (subject of يَرْجِعُونَ)
steps.n: Added هِ (him) to show who is being moved toward. This word connects to يَرْجِعُونَ (they return), showing where they return to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people are returning. This word is connected to لَعَلَّهُمْ (perhaps they) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action

steps.m: who (asking about the person who did something to our gods)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask 'who?' about people. In this ayah, it's being used to ask who did something to their gods. It connects to فَعَلَ (did) which comes right after it.

steps.m: he did (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is part of what 'they said' in the ayah.
This is a basic pointing word used to indicate something nearby. It's like pointing with your finger while saying 'this' in English.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above ه and fatha (َ) before the final alif to show the correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it's being used to point to the idols that were broken.

steps.m: to be worshipped as a deity

Changes from singular 'god' to plural 'gods' by adding آ at start and ة at end
Adds first person plural possessive pronoun
Adds preposition showing relationship to فَعَلَ (did) in the question 'Who did this to our gods?'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: indeed he (referring to the one who did this to our gods)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show we're talking about 'he' - this connects to the person mentioned in the previous part who did something to their gods

steps.m: indeed he (referring to the one who did this to our gods)

Added fatha at the end to help connect it with the next word (الظَّالِمِينَ)
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'. This word connects to الظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers) to say 'surely from the wrongdoers'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action. This word comes after مِن (from) in the sentence.

Added ال (the) at the beginning and a fatha at the end. The ظ gets a shadda because it comes after ال.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is connected to سَمِعْنَا (we heard) - they are the ones who said this.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb. The middle vowel ِ (kasra) is used because this is about sensing something.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The ْ (sukoon) is added before نَا to make it easier to pronounce. This word is part of what was said in قَالُوا (they said).
Adding vowel marks to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: a young man (who is mentioned in يَذْكُرُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added tanween (the ً mark) to show this is talking about 'a' young man, not a specific one yet known
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding prefixes and suffixes
Adds هُمْ to show who is being mentioned/remembered
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changes from past tense 'he said' to present tense 'it is said'
Added fatha to show this means 'for/to'
Added هُ at the end to show who it's for (him)
Added pronunciation helpers ۥٓ after هُ. This word refers to Ibrahim, who is mentioned right after this word in the ayah.
This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root. It's originally from Hebrew (אַבְרָהָם/Avraham) meaning 'father of many'
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject of يُقَالُ (it is said) in the ayah
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense
Changed from قَوَلَ by replacing weak letter و with ا
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word connects to فَأْتُوا۟ (so they brought) which comes next in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together
Changed from base verb 'to come' into 'I came' by adding past tense ending -tu
Added plural command ending وا to show multiple people are being addressed
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows from something before it (they said... so bring)
This is a preposition that shows how something is done. Here it connects to the command 'bring' (فَأْتُوا۟)
Added هِ which refers back to Ibrahim (إِبْرَاهِيمَ) who they are commanded to bring
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَعْيُنِ (eyes) to show something happening in front of people's eyes.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) called 'alif khanjariya' that shows this is the end of the word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'eye'
Changes singular 'eye' to plural form for small number of eyes
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Changed the root into a noun for 'people'. The أ is dropped because it's not needed in this common word.
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the ن because it's a 'sun letter' - when ٱل is added to words starting with certain letters like ن, the ل sound blends into that letter
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the word 'of' in Arabic, showing it belongs to or is connected to something
This is a word that shows you're hoping or expecting something to happen
Added هُمْ at the end to show who we're talking about. This word connects to يَشْهَدُونَ (they witness) in the next part of the ayah, showing what they might do.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now

steps.m: they witness/testify (subject of لَعَلَّهُمْ - so that they)

Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to common past tense form by adding alif

steps.m: is it you? (who did this to our gods, as asked by the people to إِبْرٰهِيمُ)
steps.n: Adding the hamza with fatha at the start turns this into a question, like adding 'is' and '?' in English. This is being asked by the people to Ibrahim about what happened to their idols.

Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action. The last vowel is replaced with a sukun (ْ) before adding تَ. In this ayah, the people are asking Ibrahim if he did something to their idols.
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts demonstrative pronouns
The alif is written as a small mark above the ه to show it's stretched when pronounced
The ذ is added to point to something specific and nearby

steps.m: this (referring to the idols they are discussing)
steps.n: The final alif makes it masculine singular, matching with the idols being discussed

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

Changed to plural form to show multiple gods
Added نَا to show these are 'our' gods
Added بِ meaning 'with'. This word is part of a question being asked to Ibrahim about what he did to their gods.

steps.m: Abraham (a prophet's name meaning 'father of many')
steps.n: This is a proper name derived from ancient Hebrew. It was the name given to the prophet Ibrahim after God promised him many descendants.

steps.m: O Abraham! (being called by his people)
steps.n: Adding يٰٓ at the start and ُ at the end changes this into a direct address - the people are calling out to Ibrahim. This form is used when directly speaking to someone.
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form. This word is connected to what comes after it, introducing what 'he' said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle

steps.m: basic word meaning: to negate or correct
steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that shows disagreement with what came before

steps.m: rather/instead (correcting what was said before in قَالَ)
steps.n: Added fatha on ب and sukun on ل to show this is correcting what was just said. In this ayah, it's correcting what was claimed in قَالَ (he said).
