steps.m: he did it (referring to breaking the idols)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what was done. The ۥ is just to help with pronunciation. This word shows what their chief idol did to the other idols.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb

steps.m: big/great one (the chief idol they are talking about)
steps.n: This form shows someone or something that has this quality strongly or permanently. In this ayah, it refers to the chief idol.
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: their chief/biggest one (referring to the idols)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show it belongs to them. In this ayah, it refers to the chief idol among their idols.
This is the basic attention-getting word used to point to something
Added ذا to specify pointing to something nearby
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to the broken idols that Ibrahim's people are discussing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to command form for one person
Added plural marker وا to make it address multiple people
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows from something before
Added هُمْ at the end to show who should be asked
This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if they could speak'. In this ayah, it's connected to the next word كَانُوا۟ to form the complete condition.

steps.m: to exist or happen in a certain state

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it 'he was'. This is the subject - the one who did the action - for 'they speak'.
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added وا with ُ before it to show many people did this action. This group is the subject (doer) of يَنطِقُونَ (they speak).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he spoke) to present tense (he speaks) by adding يَ at the start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: they speak (referring to the idols in كَانُوا)
steps.n: Masculine plural suffix changes subject from 'he' to 'they'
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he returned' in the past
Added وا to show a group of people did the action

steps.m: so they went back/returned (to أَنفُسِهِمْ - themselves)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of something before. In this ayah, they returned to themselves (أَنفُسِهِمْ) after realizing something.
This is a preposition that shows movement or direction towards something
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) above the ى which helps show it's a special kind of alif at the end. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this word connects to the next word أَنفُسِهِمْ (themselves). Together they mean 'to themselves'.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
This is a broken plural form that changes the internal structure of the word to show multiple

steps.m: themselves (referring back to the subjects of فَرَجَعُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added the attached pronoun هِمْ to show possession. This word refers back to the people who 'returned' (فَرَجَعُوٓا۟) in the ayah
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the vowels. This is common for hollow verbs (words with و or ي in the middle).
Adding وا at the end shows that a group of people did the action

steps.m: so they said (after رَجَعُوٓا۟ - they returned)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after they returned. The small marks after و (ٓ and ۟) help with proper recitation.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain

steps.m: indeed you all (addressing those who accused themselves of wrongdoing)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which shows this is speaking to multiple people. In this ayah, it's addressing the people who just realized they were wrong about Ibrahim (AS).
This is the basic pronoun for addressing one person
Adding م changes this from addressing one person to addressing many people
Adding the vowel marks shows this is the subject of the sentence. It connects to الظَّالِمُونَ (the wrongdoers) to mean 'you are the wrongdoers'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from 'he wronged' to 'wrongdoer' by adding alif after first letter - this pattern shows who does the action
Changes from one wrongdoer to many wrongdoers by adding ون at the end
Added 'al' (the) at the start and changed the ending to show it's describing 'you' in the phrase 'Indeed, you are the wrongdoers'
Basic connecting word that shows one thing happens after another
Added a small 'u' sound mark (ُ) to show this means 'then' and is a complete word

steps.m: then, next, afterwards (shows sequence of events)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to make the meaning stronger and show this connects to the next part strongly
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone else did the action to the subject

steps.m: they were turned upside down (referring to the idolaters in the story of Ibrahim)
steps.n: Added وا to show this happened to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the idolaters who were proven wrong by Ibrahim's argument.
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or above something
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else

steps.m: on, upon (preposition showing position over رُءُوسِهِمْ - their heads)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it shows their heads were physically positioned downward.
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word 'head' that we can use in a sentence
Changed from singular 'head' to broken plural 'heads' - vowels change inside the word to make it plural. Comes after عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah.
Added هِمْ to show these heads belong to them (the people being talked about in the ayah)
This word shows something has definitely happened or is definitely true
Adding لَ at the beginning makes the certainty even stronger, like saying 'most definitely' instead of just 'definitely'
Added fatha (َ) on first letter, kasra (ِ) on middle letter, and fatha (َ) on last letter to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to change 'he knew' to 'you knew'. The تَ has a stop mark (sukun) on it. This word pairs with لَقَدْ to mean 'you certainly knew'.
In this ayah, مَا is used to refer to the idols being discussed. The full context shows people admitting 'You already know these (idols) cannot speak.'
Basic demonstrative word used to point to things. Not derived from a root.
Added special letters that make it specifically refer to multiple things that are near to the speaker
Added ءِ at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَنطِقُونَ (they speak) in this ayah
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows the action is happening now

steps.m: they speak (referring to the idols who cannot speak)
steps.n: The ونَ shows multiple people or things are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the idols who are being proven unable to speak.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word introduces what he said before أَفَتَعْبُدُونَ (do you worship?).
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Adds 'ta' at start to make it 'you' (present tense) instead of 'he' (past tense)
Added ونَ to make it plural - now means multiple people are doing the action
أَ transforms the statement into a yes/no question

steps.m: Do you (all) then worship? (question to the disbelievers about their idols)
steps.n: فَ (fa) was added to mean 'then/so', connecting this question to what was said before
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects with دُونِ to mean 'besides' or 'other than'.
This is a particle word that means 'other than' or 'besides'. It's used to compare or contrast things.
Adding the damma vowel mark (ُ) helps show how to pronounce the word correctly as 'doon'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'other than Allah'
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added ال with special spelling (ٱ), doubled the ل with shadda, and added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence
This is a question word asking about what they worship. It's the object (thing being acted upon) in the phrase 'what do you worship?'
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَنفَعُكُمْ (benefits you) in this ayah, making it mean 'does not benefit you'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun after first root letter, and ُ at end

steps.m: it benefits you all (object of لا in لا يَنفَعُكُمْ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who receives the benefit. This word is negated by لا in the ayah, meaning 'does not benefit you all'
Added vowels to make it into a noun meaning 'thing'. This word is what's being affected by the verb يَنفَعُ (benefit) in this ayah - the idols cannot benefit you [in any] thing.
Added the ًا ending to show this is receiving the action of the verb. In this context, it means the idols cannot benefit you [in] anything at all.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَضُرُّكُمْ (harm you) that comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to ُ, and doubled the ر to show continuous or intense harm

steps.m: he harms you all (object of لَا in لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being harmed. This word comes after لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'does not harm you all'
Added damma and doubled the ف to create an emphatic interjection showing strong disgust

steps.m: ugh! (expression of strong disgust towards لَّكُمْ وَلِمَا تَعْبُدُونَ - you and what you worship)
steps.n: Adding tanween kasra makes this a general expression of disgust that can connect to what follows. In this ayah, it connects to 'you and what you worship'
Added fatha to make the letter pronounceable
Added the plural 'you' pronoun to show who the preposition is directed to
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This emphasis shows strong disapproval in the context of أُفٍّ (uff/fie) before it.
Starting with the basic interrogative particle 'what'. In this ayah, it refers to the false deities they worship.
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or reason - asking about why they worship these things
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement of disapproval (أُفٍّ لَّكُمْ - 'Uff to you!')
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the start to make it mean 'you' instead of 'he', and changed the vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past

steps.m: you all serve/worship (addressing the polytheists who worship idols)
steps.n: Added ون suffix to create plural masculine 'you' form
A basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects what they worship (تَعْبُدُونَ) with 'besides/other than' (دُونِ), literally meaning 'from besides Allah'

steps.m: to be below or less than something
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of being lower, beneath, or less than something else
Adding the damma (ُ) changes it from a root into a common word meaning 'below' or 'besides'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'besides Allah'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza changed to lam due to common usage
Added shadda on lam for emphasis, kasra at end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects to previous word. This word is possessed by دُونِ (besides/other than) in this ayah.
Question particle that turns a statement into a question, often used for rhetorical questions to make people think
Adds فَ to show this question follows from what was said before - in this case, asking about their lack of understanding after pointing out their wrong worship

steps.m: then will you not? (subject of تَعْقِلُونَ)
steps.n: Adds لَا to make this a negative rhetorical question. The full phrase أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ means 'then will you not use your minds?' - expressing amazement at their failure to think logically about their worship
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added ونَ to show multiple people are being addressed
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed middle weak letter و to ا, which is common for hollow verbs

steps.m: they said (subject of حَرِّقُوهُ - they said 'burn him')
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word connects to حَرِّقُوهُ (burn him) which shows what they said.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The doubled ر with kasra shows that the burning is more intense
Adding وا changes it from 'he burned' to 'you all burn!' - makes it a command for a group

steps.m: burn him intensely! (command to a group)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows who they should burn (him)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he helped' to 'help!' by adding U at start and changing middle vowel - makes it a command
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people

steps.m: and help/support! (command to a group of people)
steps.n: Added و (and) at the beginning to connect with previous phrase 'burn him!' (حَرِّقُوهُ)

steps.m: to worship or devote oneself to something

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'worship' into a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
Adding ة makes this word refer to a female deity
Adding أ at the start turns the statement into a question
Adding كم shows the goddess belongs to multiple people
This is a helper word that introduces a condition - it tells us that something will happen only if something else happens first. Like saying 'IF you study, THEN you will learn.'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command 'be!' by dropping the middle و and changing vowels - a common pattern for hollow verbs
Added تُمْ to show past tense for 'you all'. This word is connected to فٰعِلِينَ (doers) in the ayah, forming 'if you all were doers'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from the action to the person doing it by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter
Added the plural ending ين to show multiple doers. This word describes what 'you all' were in the phrase 'if you were doers'
The middle letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to ُ because this is a hollow verb (has و in middle)
Adding نا at the end changes it from 'he/it said' to 'we said'
The و (weak letter) changes to ا when making this noun form. This is a common pattern for nouns from hollow roots.
The ُ (damma) shows this word is the subject doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: O fire! (being directly addressed in قُلْنَا/We said)
steps.n: The يٰ at the beginning is used to call out to something, like saying 'Hey!' or 'O!' in English. This word is being directly addressed as part of what was said (قُلْنَا/We said).
Added ِى at the end to make it a feminine command, since it's addressing the fire (نار) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: coolness (as a command to the fire)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this is what Allah commanded the fire to become. This connects to كُونِى (be!) in the ayah.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and added alif to make it into a noun meaning peace
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon along with بَرْدًا (coolness)
Adding وَ at the start means 'and'
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or affecting something else
Added small alif (ٰ) to show elongation of final vowel sound
Added vertical fatḥa (ٓ) as an additional elongation mark to emphasize the connection with the following word إِبْرٰهِيمَ
This is the proper name Ibrahim (Abraham), a non-Arabic name that entered Arabic. It possibly comes from ancient Hebrew meaning 'father of many'.

steps.m: Ibrahim (Abraham) - as the object being addressed
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows Ibrahim is being directly affected by the action in this verse - the fire being commanded to be cool upon him.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start changes 'want' to 'intend/make want'
Adding وا shows that multiple people (they) did the action
The letter و (meaning 'and') was added to connect this action to what happened before
This is a preposition that shows how something is done. Here it connects to the verb أَرَادُوا (they intended) to show what they intended to do something with.
Adding هِ shows who they intended to do something with - in this case referring back to Prophet Ibrahim (إبراهيم)
The small ۦ after هِ is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: a plot (object of أَرَادُوا - they wanted)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to turn the action into a thing. This word is what they wanted (object of أَرَادُوا).

steps.m: a plot (object of أَرَادُوا - they wanted)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what they wanted to do (the object of their wanting)

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added vowels (َ َ َ) to the root letters to make 'he made' in the past tense
Adding نٰ changes 'he' to 'we' as the doers of the action
Adding هُمُ shows who the action was done to - 'them'

steps.m: so we made them (into ٱلْأَخْسَرِينَ - the greatest losers)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happened as a consequence of their plot mentioned in the previous part of the ayah (وَأَرَادُوا۟ بِهِۦ كَيْدًا - they intended a plot against him)

steps.m: to lose or suffer a loss

steps.n: Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Changes the verb 'lost' into 'most losing' - like turning 'lost' into 'biggest loser'. This makes it stronger and more intense.
Adds a kasra (ِ) sound to connect with the plural ending that's coming next
Makes the word plural - like changing 'biggest loser' to 'biggest losers'
Makes the word definite - like adding 'the' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added a double letter (shadda) in the middle to show doing lots of saving/rescuing, and the ending changed slightly
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who received the action (him)
Added وَ at beginning to connect this action to what came before
This is the basic form of the proper name Lot (Prophet Lut)

steps.m: Lot (as object of نَجَّيْنٰهُ - we saved)
steps.n: The ًا shows this name is receiving the action of being saved

steps.m: and Lot (as object of نَجَّيْنٰهُ - we saved)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start connects this to the previous statement, showing that Lot was also saved along with the previous subject
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement toward something. In this ayah, it connects 'We saved him' with 'the land', showing where they were saved to.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Adding kasra at the end shows possession or belonging - like saying 'of the earth'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific earth rather than any earth
Basic feminine demonstrative pronoun. The ى at the end shows it's feminine.
Adding ٱلَّ changes this from just pointing to something to connecting a description about something. Here it introduces more information about 'the land' (الْأَرْضِ) that was mentioned.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes the blessing more intense/abundant
Adding نَا changes the subject to 'we' and shows this is something Allah did
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
Added the kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: in it (referring to الْأَرْضِ - the land)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to الْأَرْضِ (the land) mentioned in the ayah. This shows what is being referred to.
Changes from 'knowing' to mean 'a sign you can see and know'
Adding alif changes it from 'sign' to mean the whole 'world' - something everyone can see and know
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple worlds or realms
Adds لِلْ (combination of لِ meaning 'for' and الْ meaning 'the')

steps.m: to give generously as a gift

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, with sukun before it

steps.m: and we gave (as a gift) (connected to previous ayah)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action to what came before
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: to/for him (connected to وَهَبْنَا - we granted)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who is receiving the action. This connects to the verb وَهَبْنَا (we granted) to show who received the granting.
Added the elongation marks ۥٓ which help with pronunciation but don't change the meaning
This is a proper name of Hebrew origin that entered Arabic through religious texts. It doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns. In this ayah, Isaac is what Allah granted to Ibrahim.

steps.m: Isaac (as direct object of وَهَبْنَا/we granted)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows that Isaac is the direct object (the one being granted) in the sentence 'We granted him Isaac'

steps.m: Jacob (as the object of وَهَبْنَا/We granted)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (recipient) of the verb 'We granted' in this ayah

steps.m: and Jacob (as the object of وَهَبْنَا/We granted)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this to the previous name إِسْحٰقَ (Isaac)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added ة at the end to make it a feminine noun meaning 'an extra thing'

steps.m: as an extra gift (object of وَهَبْنَا - we granted)
steps.n: Adds a small 'an' sound at the end to show this is receiving the action (like saying 'a gift' vs 'as a gift')
The doubled ل (shadda) shows completeness, while the damma vowel makes it a noun
Adding ًا shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلْنَا (we made)

steps.m: and all of them (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement about granting Isaac and Jacob

steps.m: to make, set, or establish something

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نا changes it from third person (he) to first person plural (we)

steps.m: to make something better or fix it

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to describe someone who has this quality
The alif is written as a small line above the ص instead of a full letter - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added ين to show we're talking about many people who do good

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Root letters got fatha vowels to make past tense 'he made'
Added sukun on ل to prepare for attachments
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هم to show who received the action (them)
Added و (and) to connect this with previous statement

steps.m: to lead or be in front

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes to broken plural pattern with doubled middle letter and ة at end
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's the object

steps.m: leaders (as the object of جَعَلْنٰهُمْ - we made them)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatha to show this word is the object of the verb 'we made them'

steps.m: to guide or show someone the right way

steps.n: The ي at start shows it's happening now and 'he' is doing it
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end is dropped before adding ون. This word describes what the leaders (أَئِمَّةً) do after Allah made them leaders.
Changed from basic root meaning 'to command' into the noun 'a command' by adding marks (fatha and sukun)
The بِ shows this is being used as a tool or means by which the guiding happens
Added 'our' and kasra before it because it follows the preposition بِ. This word shows how the guiding (يَهْدُونَ) is done - by following Allah's command.

steps.m: to inspire or reveal divine guidance

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show causing someone else to receive revelation
Added نا to show 'we' are doing the action
Added و meaning 'and' at beginning and lengthened final alif to show connection to 'to them' (إِلَيْهِمْ) that follows
This is a particle showing direction or destination
Changed ى to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun suffix

steps.m: to them (object of وَأَوْحَيْنَآ - and We inspired)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this is directed to them (masculine plural). This word receives the action of وَأَوْحَيْنَآ (and We inspired) in this ayah.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra and sukun. This makes it mean the action itself instead of doing the action.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object (thing being acted upon) of the verb يَهْدُونَ (they guide to) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Adding ات to خَيْر makes it plural and means 'good deeds' instead of just 'good'. Like adding 's' in English to make things plural, but ات is used for feminine plural words in Arabic.
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Added kasra at the end because this word is receiving the action (it's the object being done)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at start to show causing action. The middle و changed to ا because it's a weak letter
Changed أ to إِ to make it a command
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and'. This word connects to 'prayer' (ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to pray or connect with something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the weak letter و to ا and added ة to make it a noun for a single instance of prayer
The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is connected to another word after it
Added ال to make it definite
Changed the ending to show this word is receiving the action, with a small i-sound (kasra) at the end
Basic meaning of bringing or coming
Changes the verb 'to come/bring' into a noun meaning 'the act of giving/bringing' by adding إِ at start and ء at end

steps.m: giving/bringing (as a noun in accusative case)
steps.n: Adds a fatḥa mark at the end to show this noun is in the accusative case (like an object)
Adds 'and' at the beginning to connect to previous phrase. The fatha at the end shows this is the object (thing receiving the action) of the verb أَوْحَيْنَآ (we inspired/revealed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the final و to ا and added ة to make it a noun meaning the act of purifying through giving
Added ال to make it definite, with the ز getting a shadda because it comes after ال
Changed spelling from ا to وٰ and added kasra (i sound) at the end because it's connected to the word 'giving of' before it
Added vowel marks to make past tense. The middle و becomes ا in past tense because it's a weak letter.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action in the past
Added 'and' (و) at the start to connect with 'worshippers' later, showing they were worshippers
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This is a particle showing something is for or belongs to someone.
Added نَا which shows this refers to 'us'. In this ayah, it connects to عٰبِدِينَ (worshippers) to show we were worshippers.
This pattern shows someone who regularly or professionally does the action. The alif after the first root letter shows this is someone's role or characteristic.
Makes the word plural and shows it's affected by كَانُوا (they were). The ين ending shows both plurality and that it's being acted upon by كَانُوا.
Added proper diacritical marks to show exact pronunciation. The alif is written with a special mark (عٰ) in this style.
This is a proper name, so we start with the word itself rather than a root
Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and Lot (object of ءَاتَيْنٰهُ - we gave)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show that Lot is receiving the action of being given wisdom and knowledge
Connected the root letters together
Changed to Form IV pattern by adding alif after hamza. This makes the action affect someone else.
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding suffixes
Added نَا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who received the action (him)
Connected the root letters together
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the result of judging - wisdom

Added كُمْ at the end to show this belongs to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: to worship or devote oneself completely to something

steps.n: Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The alif is written as a small mark above to show long 'aa' sound
Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a deity' instead of 'the deity'). This word is described by وٰحِدٌ (one) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be alone, to be unique

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'one who is unique/alone'
Adds tanween dammah to make it 'a single one' instead of 'the single one'

steps.m: one, single (describing إِلٰهٌ 'god' in this ayah)
steps.n: The alif after و became small (ٰ), but the word still means 'one' and describes 'god' in this ayah
This combines فَ (so/then) with هَلْ (question word for yes/no questions)

Adding م with damma makes it refer to multiple people being addressed (in this ayah, it's addressing all people who hear/read this message)
Changes from verb 'submit' to noun meaning 'submitter'

steps.m: those who submit/surrender (to Allah) (answering: فَهَلْ أَنتُم - 'then are you?')
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people. This word answers the question 'then are you?' in the ayah.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations. It's similar to saying 'if' in English when talking about possible situations.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what comes before it. In this ayah, it connects to the word تَوَلَّوْا (they turn away) that follows it.

steps.m: to turn away or take charge of something

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he turned away' in the past tense
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or complete
Adding تَ shows the person is doing the action to themselves

steps.m: they turned themselves away (from the truth)
steps.n: Adding وا shows a group of people did this action
The middle letter و drops out when making a command, and we add damma and sukun to show it's a command
The فَ shows this command follows from what came before
Added alif after first letter to make the verb more emphatic
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added كُمْ at the end to show the announcement was directed to multiple people
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or over something else
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show the long 'aa' sound. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: equally (describing how the announcement in ءَاذَنتُكُمْ was made)

This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a possible situation, like saying 'if' in English
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb. The final ى replaces ي because it's at the end of the word.

steps.n: This particle introduces the second part of a question, connecting it to the first option أَقَرِيبٌ (is it near). It's like asking 'is it near or far?' in English.
Added ي in the middle and vowel marks to make it into a word describing distance

steps.m: far away (referring to something not specified yet)
steps.n: The ٌ at the end shows we're talking about something far away in general, not a specific far thing
Basic form of the relative pronoun ما which refers to something unknown or general. Here it refers to what is being promised.
Added shadda and fatha for emphasis. This emphasizes the questioning nature of 'what' in this context, as it's part of a question about what is being promised.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' to a sentence

steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who the sentence is about. This 'he' is the one who 'knows' (يَعْلَمُ) in the next part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb

steps.m: he knows (what is said openly in ٱلْجَهْرَ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on ع, and adding ُ at the end. This verb tells what Allah knows about what is said openly.

steps.m: open speech (object of يَعْلَمُ - he knows)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning the act itself. This word is what is being known (object) in the phrase 'he knows the open speech'.

steps.m: the open speech (object of يَعْلَمُ - he knows)
steps.n: Adds 'the' at start and accusative case ending showing this word is object
This is a helper word that connects things, showing something comes from or is part of something else. Here it connects to 'the speech' (ٱلْقَوْلِ)
When مِنْ comes before a word starting with ال, we change the ْ to َ to make it flow better when speaking
Changed from action to the thing itself. This word is what is being known in the phrase 'he knows the speech'

steps.m: the speech (object of يَعْلَمُ - he knows)
steps.n: Added 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' + kasra at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changes from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - like changing 'knew' to 'knows'

steps.m: and he knows (connected to يَعْلَمُ in previous ayah)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this statement to the previous يَعْلَمُ, showing Allah knows both what is spoken openly and what is hidden
This is a word that shows what is being hidden in the phrase 'what you conceal.' It connects to the verb تَكْتُمُونَ (you conceal/hide) by being its object - the thing that is being concealed.
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha marks

steps.m: you (all) hide (something secretly) (subject of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Changed from 'you hide' (singular) to 'you all hide' (plural) - shows multiple people are hiding something
This is a conditional particle that introduces uncertainty. In this ayah, it shows uncertainty about whether the speaker knows something.

steps.m: and if/whether (introduces uncertainty about أَدْرِى/I know)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to what came before it, while إِنْ introduces uncertainty about the knowledge mentioned
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: I know (in response to وَإِنْ - and if)
steps.n: Changed to first person present tense by adding أَ at beginning and changing vowels. This word comes after وَإِنْ (and if) to express uncertainty.
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation

steps.m: perhaps he/it (referring to what was mentioned)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show who the word is talking about. This connects back to something mentioned before.

Added ة to show this is one specific instance of testing

Added the second person masculine plural pronoun suffix

Added two dots above the end (tanween) to make it mean 'a/an' instead of 'the'

Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to pass (time), to arrive (of a time)

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis, making it into a noun meaning 'truth'
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the truth' instead of just 'truth'
Added بِ at the start to show how something is done - in this case, judging with or by the truth
Added kasra at the end because this word is the object (thing being judged with) after the command 'judge' (احْكُم)

Adds ٰن to show someone who has a lot of this quality (mercy)

Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it describes رَبُّنَا (our Lord) in this ayah
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of, against, or over something else
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end, which is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning. This preposition connects to the verb تَصِفُونَ (you describe) that comes after it.
This is a question word that asks about something unknown. Here it's asking about what they are describing/attributing. It's the object of the verb تَصِفُونَ (you describe/attribute).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start of a present tense verb.
